item.title: TRE Python binding — ReDoS robustness demo 摘要
item.description:
Simon Willison explores TRE, a regular expression engine by Ville Laurikari that avoids backtracking, making it robust against ReDoS attacks. He built an experimental Python binding using ctypes and tested it against malicious regex patterns. TRE handled these attacks much better than Python's standard library re module, which relies on backtracking and is vulnerable to catastrophic backtracking. The work was inspired by antirez adding TRE to Redis. The binding is available on GitHub for demonstration purposes.
标签: security, python, regular-expressions, c, ctypes, ReDoS, TRE, backtracking, Redis, open-source

item.title: Anthropic Executive, One Year Ago: Fully AI Employees Are a Year Away 摘要
item.description:
A year ago, Anthropic's CISO Jason Clinton predicted that AI-powered virtual employees with their own memories, roles, and corporate accounts would soon roam corporate networks. However, this prediction has not materialized. The article argues that this was not a genuine warning but an advertisement aimed at executives eager to replace human workers with AI. Such hype has contributed to negative public sentiment about AI, as companies are not actually deploying fully autonomous AI employees and likely should not.
标签: AI, Anthropic, virtual employees, AI agents, corporate automation, AI hype, public sentiment, security, autonomous systems, workforce

item.title: Photoshop’s ‘Modern User Interface’ Sucks (and Doesn’t Feel Modern) 摘要
item.description:
Marcin Wichary criticizes Adobe Photoshop's 'modern user interface' redesign, arguing it removes useful UI elements and degrades the user experience. He contrasts before-and-after screenshots showing worse balance, focus behavior, and typography in the new version. Wichary attributes this to carelessness and laziness in UI engineering, not to necessary evolution or technical challenges. He compares it to similar critiques of Apple's app icon design regression, suggesting the changes feel like a step backward rather than an improvement.
标签: Photoshop, UI design, Adobe, software regression, user experience, interface redesign, critique, design quality, power users

item.title: ‘Noir, Japan’s Hard-Boiled Bittersweet Answer to Oreos’ 摘要
item.description:
Jake Adelstein reports that Japan's Oreos were originally made domestically by Yamazaki Biscuits under license from Mondelez. When the license expired, Mondelez moved production to China, altering the taste. In response, Yamazaki launched Noir in December 2017, a cookie that replicated the original Oreo flavor. John Gruber, a fan of Newman-O's, finds Noir intriguing but notes it's hard to get in the U.S.
标签: Oreo, Japan, Yamazaki Biscuits, Mondelez, Noir, cookie, food, business, licensing, consumer

item.title: App Store Search Ads and the Slippery Slope 摘要
item.description:
Jeremy Provost reports that iOS App Store search has shifted from relevance to ad inventory, with Apple's second search ad pushing organic results down. His apps saw 23-30% decreases in search impressions, except one using Search Ads which increased 3%. The article applies the Zero-One-Infinity rule to ads, arguing that once Apple showed more than one ad, it's on a slippery slope toward infinite ads, citing upcoming ads in Apple Maps as further evidence.
标签: iOS, App Store, search ads, Apple, mobile apps, advertising, user experience, slippery slope, Zero-One-Infinity rule

item.title: Google Owns a Big Chunk of Anthropic 摘要
item.description:
The New York Times reports that Google owns a 14% stake in AI startup Anthropic, with a cap of 15% ownership and no voting rights or board seats. Google has invested over $3 billion in Anthropic, including a $750 million convertible debt due in September. Anthropic's latest Series G funding round valued the company at $380 billion, making Google's stake worth over $50 billion. This investment provides Google a strategic hedge, benefiting from both its own Gemini AI and Anthropic's Claude, while contributing to an 81% increase in Google's overall profit.
标签: Google, Anthropic, AI, investment, antitrust, startup, funding, convertible debt, valuation, Stratechery

item.title: AI didn't delete your database, you did 摘要
item.description:
A viral tweet claimed a Cursor/Claude agent deleted a company's production database, sparking debate about AI accountability. The author argues the real issue is having an API endpoint that can delete the entire production database, not the AI itself. He recounts a personal story from 2010 where he accidentally deleted a trunk folder during a manual deployment, leading to automation improvements. He compares AI to manual processes prone to errors, noting that AI lacks true reasoning and is just generating tokens. The solution is to know what you deploy, use AI as a tool for competent developers, and avoid letting non-developers write code.
标签: AI, accountability, software engineering, deployment, automation, vibe coding, production database, mistakes, CI/CD, tooling

item.title: April 2026 newsletter 摘要
item.description:
Simon Willison released the April 2026 edition of his sponsors-only monthly newsletter, accessible to GitHub sponsors. The newsletter covers Opus 4.7 and GPT-5.5 with price increases, Claude Mythos and LLM security research, ChatGPT Images 2.0, more model releases, blog highlights, and his April 2026 setup. A preview of the March newsletter is available. Sponsorship costs $10/month for early access.
标签: newsletter, AI, LLM, GPT, Claude, ChatGPT, security, model releases, sponsors, Simon Willison

item.title: ★ Y Combinator’s Stake in OpenAI 摘要
item.description:
John Gruber discusses a potential conflict of interest regarding Paul Graham's defense of Sam Altman. Y Combinator, where Graham is a founding partner, reportedly owns about 0.6% of OpenAI, worth over $5 billion at OpenAI's $852 billion valuation. Gruber argues this financial stake should have been disclosed when Graham was quoted as a character reference for Altman in a New Yorker investigation, as it could bias his opinion on Altman's trustworthiness.
标签: OpenAI, Sam Altman, Y Combinator, Paul Graham, conflict of interest, journalism, ethics, AI, valuation, disclosure

item.title: Quoting Andy Masley 摘要
item.description:
Andy Masley argues that data center land use concerns are exaggerated. Between 2000 and 2024, farmers voluntarily sold a Colorado-sized area of land (77 times the projected data center land use in 2028) while increasing food production. He highlights the irony of a farmer selling a few acres of mediocre hay field for ten times its agricultural value, triggering claims of farmland loss despite no impact on US food access.
标签: data centers, land use, agriculture, AI, myths, environment, technology, food production, urban development

item.title: Paul Thurrott Might Write a Book on Markdown 摘要
item.description:
Paul Thurrott is considering writing a short e-book about Markdown, possibly as part of a monthly focus. He has already drafted an introductory chapter tentatively titled 'On writing.' The author reflects on the parallel but seldom intersecting careers of Thurrott and himself, noting that this book project is an unexpected but welcome development, preferable to writing about something Thurrott created in 2004.
标签: Markdown, e-book, writing, Paul Thurrott, publishing, technology, blogging, career
